_comment:
Synthetic frame sequence for OcclusionHandler + HandIdentityModule tests. Each frame lists 0, 1, or 2 hands with their wrist (x, y) positions and chirality. Wrist is the only field required by HandIdentityModule.assign_roles(). Times are in seconds; the sequence is designed so that:
  - Frame 0 (t=0.000): two hands, left at x=0.2, right at x=0.8 -> roles HAND_A, HAND_B assigned
  - Frame 1 (t=0.033): two hands still present -> role continuity preserved
  - Frame 2 (t=0.066): right hand disappears -> right-hand should be BRIDGED (within 300ms)
  - Frame 3 (t=0.166): right hand still missing -> still bridged (166ms < 300ms)
  - Frame 4 (t=0.366): right hand still missing -> bridge RELEASES (366ms > 300ms)
  - Frame 5 (t=0.466): right hand reappears at x=0.8 -> HandIdentityModule reassigns via proximity
  - Frame 6 (t=0.566): hand-crossing test -- left hand now at x=0.75, right hand at x=0.25 (swapped). Roles preserved across crossing.
  - Frame 7 (t=0.666): hands back to original positions -> roles preserved.
